question: [File: 'src/libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Periphery integration bug that can cause user loss when interacting with the core Midnight contract as intended'] [Function: safeTransferFrom / MidnightBundles.buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral] Can an unprivileged taker using a fee-on-transfer loan token under the precondition that the token deducts a fee on every transferFrom call trigger the sequence pullToken(loanToken, msg.sender, maxBuyerAssets) -> forceApproveMax -> take loop (Midnight pulls filledBuyerAssets from bundler) -> safeTransfer(loanToken, msg.sender, maxBuyerAssets - filledBuyerAssets - referralFeeAssets), violating the invariant that the bundler's actual token balance after pullToken equals maxBuyerAssets, causing scoped impact: the refund safeTransfer reverts because bundler holds maxBuyerAssets*(1-fee) - filledBuyerAssets which is less than the computed refund amount, permanently DoS-ing the bundle for that token? Proof idea: deploy a fee-on-transfer ERC20 with configurable fee bps; fuzz fee in [1, 9999]; assert that buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral reverts with a transfer failure rather than OutOfOffers when bundler has no residual balance.

[File: 'src/libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Periphery integration bug that can cause user loss when interacting with the core Midnight contract as intended'] [Function: safeTransferFrom / MidnightBundles.buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral] Can an unprivileged taker using a fee-on-transfer loan token under the precondition that the MidnightBundles contract holds a residual loan token balance R from a prior transaction trigger the sequence pullToken(loanToken, msg.sender, maxBuyerAssets) -> take loop -> safeTransfer(loanToken, msg.sender, maxBuyerAssets - filledBuyerAssets - referralFeeAssets), violating the invariant that the bundler must not transfer more loan tokens than it received in the current call, causing scoped impact: the refund succeeds by consuming R from the bundler's residual balance, draining funds belonging to a prior user? Proof idea: seed bundler with residual balance R; call buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral with fee-on-transfer token; assert bundler balance decreases by more than the fee charged on the current pullToken.

[File: 'src/libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Periphery integration bug that can cause user loss when interacting with the core Midnight contract as intended'] [Function: safeTransferFrom / MidnightBundles.supplyCollateralAndSellWithUnitsTarget] Can an unprivileged taker using a fee-on-transfer loan token under the precondition that Midnight sends sellerAssets to address(this) (bundler) via safeTransferFrom(loanToken, payer, bundler, sellerAssets) trigger the sequence supplyCollateral -> take loop (Midnight transfers sellerAssets to bundler) -> safeTransfer(loanToken, receiver, filledSellerAssets - referralFeeAssets), violating the invariant that bundler's received sellerAssets equals filledSellerAssets, causing scoped impact: the final safeTransfer to receiver reverts because bundler holds filledSellerAssets*(1-fee) which is less than filledSellerAssets - referralFeeAssets when fee > referralFeePct/WAD? Proof idea: fuzz fee bps and referralFeePct; assert revert condition fee * filledSellerAssets > referralFeeAssets.

[File: 'src/libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Periphery integration bug that can cause user loss when interacting with the core Midnight contract as intended'] [Function: safeTransferFrom / MidnightBundles.supplyCollateralAndSellWithUnitsTarget] Can an unprivileged taker using a fee-on-transfer loan token under the precondition that the bundler holds residual loan token balance R trigger the sequence take loop (Midnight sends filledSellerAssets to bundler, bundler receives filledSellerAssets*(1-fee)) -> safeTransfer(loanToken, receiver, filledSellerAssets - referralFeeAssets), violating the invariant that receiver must not receive more than what Midnight actually transferred to the bundler in this call, causing scoped impact: receiver receives filledSellerAssets - referralFeeAssets which exceeds filledSellerAssets*(1-fee), with the shortfall drawn from bundler's residual balance belonging to other users? Proof idea: seed bundler with R; call supplyCollateralAndSellWithUnitsTarget with fee-on-transfer loan token; assert receiver_received > midnight_sent_to_bundler.

[File: 'src/libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Periphery integration bug that can cause user loss when interacting with the core Midnight contract as intended'] [Function: safeTransferFrom / MidnightBundles.supplyCollateralAndSellWithUnitsTarget] Can an unprivileged taker using a fee-on-transfer collateral token under the precondition that the token deducts a fee on transferFrom trigger the sequence pullToken(collateralToken, msg.sender, assets) -> forceApproveMax(collateralToken, MIDNIGHT) -> supplyCollateral(market, index, assets, taker) -> Midnight.safeTransferFrom(collateralToken, bundler, address(this), assets), violating the invariant that Midnight must receive exactly assets collateral tokens, causing scoped impact: Midnight's safeTransferFrom reverts because bundler holds assets*(1-fee) < assets, making the entire bundle revert and DoS-ing the user's intended borrow flow? Proof idea: deploy fee-on-transfer collateral token; assert supplyCollateralAndSellWithUnitsTarget reverts at the supplyCollateral step.

[File: 'src/libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Periphery integration bug that can cause user loss when interacting with the core Midnight contract as intended'] [Function: safeTransferFrom / MidnightBundles.repayAndWithdrawCollateral] Can an unprivileged borrower using a fee-on-transfer loan token under the precondition that the token charges fee f on every transferFrom trigger the sequence pullToken(loanToken, msg.sender, assets) -> forceApproveMax -> repay(market, units=assets-referralFeeAssets, onBehalf) -> Midnight.safeTransferFrom(loanToken, bundler, address(this), units) -> safeTransfer(loanToken, referralFeeRecipient, referralFeeAssets), violating the invariant that bundler's balance after pullToken equals assets, causing scoped impact: Midnight's repay pull of units from bundler fails because bundler holds assets*(1-f) < units when f*assets > referralFeeAssets, DoS-ing the repay flow? [File: 'src/libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Periphery integration bug that can cause user loss when interacting with the core Midnight contract as intended'] [Function: safeTransferFrom / MidnightBundles.repayAndWithdrawCollateral] Can an unprivileged borrower using a fee-on-transfer loan token under the precondition that bundler holds residual balance R trigger the sequence pullToken(loanToken, msg.sender, assets) -> repay(market, units, onBehalf) -> safeTransfer(loanToken, referralFeeRecipient, referralFeeAssets), violating the invariant that total tokens leaving bundler in this call must not exceed tokens entering bundler in this call, causing scoped impact: Midnight pulls units from bundler and bundler sends referralFeeAssets to recipient, total outflow = units + referralFeeAssets = assets, but bundler only received assets*(1-f), so shortfall f*assets is drawn from residual balance R, draining prior users' funds? [File: 'src/libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Periphery integration bug that can cause user loss when interacting with the core Midnight contract as intended'] [Function: safeTransferFrom / MidnightBundles.buyWithAssetsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral] Can an unprivileged taker using a fee-on-transfer loan token under the precondition that the token charges fee f trigger the sequence pullToken(loanToken, msg.sender, targetBuyerAssets) -> forceApproveMax -> take loop (Midnight pulls filledBuyerAssets from bundler) -> safeTransfer(loanToken, referralFeeRecipient, referralFeeAssets), violating the invariant that bundler holds targetBuyerAssets after pullToken, causing scoped impact: Midnight's safeTransferFrom of filledBuyerAssets from bundler fails because bundler holds targetBuyerAssets*(1-f) < filledBuyerAssets when f is large enough, DoS-ing the buy flow? [File: 'src/libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Periphery integration bug that can cause user loss when interacting with the core Midnight contract as intended'] [Function: safeTransferFrom / MidnightBundles.supplyCollateralAndSellWithAssetsTarget] Can an unprivileged taker using a fee-on-transfer loan token under the precondition that Midnight sends filledSellerAssets = targetSellerAssets + referralFeeAssets to bundler trigger the sequence take loop (Midnight sends filledSellerAssets to bundler, bundler receives filledSellerAssets*(1-f)) -> safeTransfer(loanToken, referralFeeRecipient, referralFeeAssets) -> safeTransfer(loanToken, receiver, targetSellerAssets), violating the invariant that bundler's outflow equals bundler's inflow from Midnight, causing scoped impact: total outflow referralFeeAssets + targetSellerAssets = filledSellerAssets but bundler holds filledSellerAssets*(1-f), causing revert or residual balance drain? [File: 'src/libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Periphery integration bug that can cause user loss when interacting with the core Midnight contract as intended'] [Function: safeTransfer / Midnight.withdraw] Can an unprivileged lender using a fee-on-transfer loan token under the precondition that the token charges fee f on every transfer trigger the sequence withdraw(market, units, onBehalf, receiver) -> position[id][onBehalf].credit -= units -> marketState[id].withdrawable -= units -> safeTransfer(loanToken, receiver, units), violating the invariant that receiver must receive exactly units loan tokens, causing scoped impact: receiver receives units*(1-f) < units while Midnight's accounting deducted units from credit and withdrawable, creating a permanent discrepancy between accounting and actual token balance? [File: 'src/libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Periphery integration bug that can cause user loss when interacting with the core Midnight contract as intended'] [Function: safeTransferFrom / Midnight.repay] Can an unprivileged borrower using a fee-on-transfer loan token under the precondition that the token charges fee f trigger the sequence repay(market, units, onBehalf, callback=address(0)) -> position[id][onBehalf].debt -= units -> marketState[id].withdrawable += units -> safeTransferFrom(loanToken, msg.sender, address(this), units), violating the invariant that Midnight's loan token balance must increase by exactly units, causing scoped impact: Midnight receives units*(1-f) but records withdrawable += units, making the protocol insolvent by f*units per repay call? [File: 'src/libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Periphery integration bug that can cause user loss when interacting with the core Midnight contract as intended'] [Function: safeTransferFrom / Midnight.supplyCollateral] Can an unprivileged borrower using a fee-on-transfer collateral token under the precondition that the token charges fee f trigger the sequence supplyCollateral(market, index, assets, onBehalf) -> position[id][onBehalf].collateral[index] += assets -> safeTransferFrom(collateralToken, msg.sender, address(this), assets), violating the invariant that Midnight's collateral balance must equal the sum of all position.collateral[index] values, causing scoped impact: Midnight records assets collateral but holds assets*(1-f), so on liquidation the seized collateral transfer safeTransfer(collateralToken, receiver, seizedAssets) may revert or drain other users' collateral? [File: 'src/libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Periphery integration bug that can cause user loss when interacting with the core Midnight contract as intended'] [Function: safeTransfer / Midnight.withdrawCollateral] Can an unprivileged borrower using a fee-on-transfer collateral token under the precondition that the token charges fee f trigger the sequence withdrawCollateral(market, index, assets, onBehalf, receiver) -> position[id][onBehalf].collateral[index] -= assets -> isHealthy check passes -> safeTransfer(collateralToken, receiver, assets), violating the invariant that receiver must receive exactly assets collateral tokens, causing scoped impact: receiver receives assets*(1-f) while Midnight deducted assets from collateral accounting, causing user loss of f*assets per withdrawal? [File: 'src/libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Periphery integration bug that can cause user loss when interacting with the core Midnight contract as intended'] [Function: safeTransfer / Midnight.liquidate] Can an unprivileged liquidator using a fee-on-transfer collateral token under the precondition that the token charges fee f trigger the sequence liquidate(market, index, seizedAssets, repaidUnits, borrower, ...) -> safeTransfer(collateralToken, receiver, seizedAssets) -> onLiquidate callback -> safeTransferFrom(loanToken, payer, address(this), repaidUnits), violating the invariant that the liquidator receives exactly seizedAssets collateral, causing scoped impact: liquidator receives seizedAssets*(1-f) making liquidation economically unprofitable and causing liquidation failure for fee-on-transfer collateral tokens? [File: 'src/libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Periphery integration bug that can cause user loss when interacting with the core Midnight contract as intended'] [Function: safeTransfer + safeTransferFrom / Midnight.take] Can an unprivileged taker using a fee-on-transfer loan token under the precondition that the token charges fee f trigger the sequence take(offer, ...) -> safeTransferFrom(loanToken, payer, address(this), buyerAssets - sellerAssets) -> safeTransferFrom(loanToken, payer, receiver, sellerAssets), violating the invariant that claimableSettlementFee[loanToken] += buyerAssets - sellerAssets must be backed by actual token balance, causing scoped impact: Midnight records full settlement fee but receives (buyerAssets - sellerAssets)*(1-f), creating insolvency in claimableSettlementFee that grows with each take? [File: 'src/libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Periphery integration bug that can cause user loss when interacting with the core Midnight contract as intended'] [Function: safeTransfer + safeTransferFrom / Midnight.flashLoan] Can an unprivileged flash loan user using a fee-on-transfer token under the precondition that the token charges fee f trigger the sequence flashLoan([token], [assets], callback, data) -> safeTransfer(token, callback, assets) -> onFlashLoan callback -> safeTransferFrom(token, callback, address(this), assets), violating the invariant that Midnight's token balance must be unchanged after a flash loan, causing scoped impact: Midnight sends assets but receives assets*(1-f) back, losing f*assets per flash loan call, draining the protocol's token reserves?
